original_abstract: In this letter, we propose a practical non-linear energy harvesting (EH) model and develop a corresponding resource allocation algorithm for simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) systems. Unlike traditional linear models that assume a fixed power conversion efficiency, our approach accounts for the physical limitations and saturation effects of real-world EH circuits. We formulate the resource allocation strategy as a non-convex optimization problem aimed at maximizing the total power harvested by energy receivers while guaranteeing minimum signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) requirements for multiple information receivers. To solve this, we transform the sum-of-ratios objective function into an equivalent subtractive form, enabling an efficient iterative optimization process. Within each iteration, we employ semidefinite relaxation to solve a rank-constrained semidefinite program (SDP) optimally. Numerical results demonstrate that designing resource allocation based on this non-linear model yields significant performance gains over conventional linear models, which often suffer from resource mismatches in practical deployments.
humanized_abstract: In this letter, we introduce a practical non-linear EH model and its corresponding resource allocation algorithm for SWIPT systems. Unlike the conventional linear EH model, the non-linear EH model is based on the practical non-linear EH circuit and considers its non-linear saturation region. With the proposed non-linear EH model, the resource allocation problem is formulated into a non-convex optimization problem, which maximizes the energy received by the energy receivers (ERs) and ensures the sufficient signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) of the information receivers (IRs). The sum-of-ratios function is transformed into an equivalent subtractive form via convex modification techniques, which enables the design of an efficient iterative optimization algorithm. In each iteration, an optimal solution for the rank-constrained semidefinite program (SDP) can be obtained by solving a corresponding feasible SDP. Simulation results show that the proposed non-linear resource allocation has a better performance compared with linear resource allocation, which can alleviate the mismatch of resources in practice.
